perturbation.original_text: Schoolpop shall comply with all applicable Federal, State and local laws concerning the use of Cards, including anti-money laundering and anti-terrorism laws, and currency control regulations. Schoolpop warrants it shall take measures to track information about its Clients which shall meet compliance requirements related to the above stated laws, track all Card shipment(s) to each such Client, and maintain appropriate records for its Clients for a period of seven (7) years after termination of the Agreement and to provide such records and other information to AEIS upon reasonable request and subject to applicable law.
perturbation.changed_text: Schoolpop shall comply with all applicable Federal, State and local laws concerning the use of Cards, including anti-money laundering and anti-terrorism laws, and currency control regulations. Schoolpop warrants it shall take measures to track information about its Clients which shall meet compliance requirements related to the above stated laws, track all Card shipment(s) to each such Client, and maintain appropriate records for its Clients for a period of two (2) years after termination of the Agreement and to provide such records and other information to AEIS upon reasonable request and subject to applicable law.
perturbation.explanation: The change reduces the record-keeping duration to two years. This may not comply with regulations that require longer retention periods.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 31 CFR § 1020.220 Customer identification program for banks.
perturbation.law_explanation: This regulation requires banks to retain records of customer identification for five years after the account is closed, which is longer than the two years specified in the modified contract text. The two year limit could limit the ability to provide required information to law enforcement or other regulatory bodies.

perturbation.justification: The modified contract reduces the record-keeping duration from seven years to two years, which contradicts the Law Explanation stating that regulations require banks to retain customer identification records for five years after the account is closed. Scraped Law Snippet 1 refers to 'eCFR :: 31 CFR 1020.220 -- Customer identification program requirements for banks.' While the specific retention period isn't in the snippet, it confirms the existence of regulations concerning customer identification programs for banks, suggesting that record retention requirements are legally mandated, as explained in the Law Explanation. The change directly reduces the legally required time, thus creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each policy required hereunder shall provide that AEIS shall receive thirty (30) days advance written notice in the event of a cancellation or material change in such policy.
perturbation.changed_text: Each policy required hereunder shall provide that AEIS shall receive ten (10) days advance written notice in the event of a cancellation or material change in such policy.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the notice period to 10 days may violate state regulations requiring a minimum 30-day notification for insurance policy changes.
perturbation.law_explanation: This code mandates a thirty-day notice period for cancellation of insurance policies. By shortening the notification timeline to ten days, the modified contract potentially violates this statute and makes the contract non-compliant with Georgia state law, where Schoolpop, Inc. is located. Schoolpop would not have adequate time to procure alternative insurance coverage, potentially exposing AEIS to uncovered liability.

perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the advance notice period from 30 days to 10 days. The Perturbation Explanation states this may violate regulations requiring a minimum 30-day notification. The Law Explanation further clarifies that this violates Georgia state law, where Schoolpop, Inc. is located, and that a 30-day notice period is mandated. The original text adheres to the standard, while the changed text directly contradicts this requirement, creating a clear violation, as the inconsistency definition describes.
